Prediction System Notes

How The Predictor Works

Loading run details...

Back to prediction bounties

Accuracy Snapshot

These metrics are calculated from historical prediction rows exported by the same daily pipeline. Win/loss grading is based on OVER/UNDER direction versus the posted market line, with pushes tracked separately.

By Target

By Direction

Pipeline Overview

The predictor is a market-aware decision pipeline. Each daily run refreshes NBA game logs, ingests current prop lines, aligns historical rows, builds a current market snapshot, ranks candidates, then exports a publishable board.

Candidate rows are scored with model outputs and policy controls. The board optimizer then applies portfolio constraints so the final selection balances edge, risk concentration, and support quality instead of maximizing one row in isolation.

The published board is not a raw model dump. It is the result of ranking, calibration, and governance layers that enforce limits by player, target, and game while controlling low-support or contradictory plays.

How A Play Is Built

  1. The model estimates a player stat outcome for a target market such as PTS, TRB, or AST.
  2. The prediction is compared against the market line to produce signed edge and direction (OVER or UNDER).
  3. Expected win rate and EV are calibrated and adjusted by policy-level confidence controls.
  4. Risk overlays apply concentration and uncertainty penalties before final board ranking.
  5. Portfolio caps enforce practical limits so one player, game, or script cannot dominate the board.

What The Metrics Mean

  • Win Rate: wins divided by graded plays (pushes excluded).
  • Push Rate: pushes divided by settled plays (win/loss/push only).
  • ROI / Graded Play: unit return per graded play, using -110 pricing assumptions.
  • MAE: mean absolute error between predicted stat and actual stat for settled rows.
  • RMSE: root mean squared error; emphasizes larger misses.
  • Mean Absolute Edge: average absolute difference between prediction and line.
